title: lotus pedunculatus subsp. pedunculatus
intro: Lotus pedunculatus subsp. pedunculatus, also known as the common meadow grass or meadow vetchling, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to Europe and western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis and gastrointestinal issues. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Lotus pedunculatus subsp. pedunculatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with the dried flowers, leaves, and roots being used to extract its medicinal properties.
